finding: F-B07-RA-1: atom_type=HEADING but PDF shows 'b. RFXSTDTC and RFXENDTC:' as a lettered LIST_ITEM under item 5 RFSTDTC/RFENDTC paired-variables discussion (a/b/c/d series, a=RFSTDTC and RFENDTC, b=RFXSTDTC and RFXENDTC, c=RFCSTDTC and RFCENDTC, d=RFICDTC and RFPENDTC); also verbatim truncates the remainder of item b (loses 'RFXSTDTC and RFXENDTC always represent the date/time of first and last study exposure...' through '...for the last treatment element described in the SE dataset.'); also parent_section='§5 [General Observation Classes]' is wrong chapter — page 67 is DM Assumptions continuation within §5.2 [Demographics (DM)] (not the §5 class-level heading and not §5 General Observation Classes from ch.3). 4/4 checks FAIL.

F-B07-RA-2: parent_section='§5.2 [Demographics (DM)]' is wrong. This is the co.xpt table header (Row|STUDYID|DOMAIN|RDOMAIN|USUBJID|COSEQ|IDVAR|IDVARVAL|COREF|COVAL|COVAL1|COVAL2|COEVAL|VISITNUM|CODTC) which appears at the TOP of p.62 as the tail of §5.1 [Comments (CO)] examples. §5.2 [Demographics (DM)] heading appears BELOW the co.xpt table on p.62 (mid-page). The CO example table belongs under §5.1, not §5.2. Classic section-boundary misattribution (echoes O-P1-12 page misattribution concern for spec tables near section boundaries). Verbatim + atom_type correct; parent_section mis-assigned to the next section on same page.

F-B07-RA-3: minor verbatim deviation — atom says 'See also Section 5.3, Subject Elements, Example 2' with capital 'See', but PDF p.65 item 4.b reads '...; see also Section 5.3, Subject Elements, Example 2.' (lowercase 'see' mid-sentence with leading semicolon and trailing period). Semantic target + cross_refs=['§5.3'] correct. Non-semantic capitalization fix needed.
